authors.name: RelaxTouch Editorial Team
authors.bio_html: The RelaxTouch Editorial Team is a small group of licensed massage therapists and wellness editors who write, fact-check and review every guide on this site. We draw on decades of combined hands-on practice and a small library of peer-reviewed bodywork research. If you spot an error or have a topic you'd like us to cover, we'd love to hear from you.

pages.title: About RelaxTouch
pages.meta_description: RelaxTouch operates premium wellness massage studios in 15 U.S. cities, staffed by licensed therapists and built around a single idea: real recovery, in a beautiful room.
pages.content_html:
RelaxTouch is a small network of premium wellness massage studios designed for one thing: to give your body — and your nervous system — a quiet hour back.

We started with a simple observation. Most massage in the United States falls into two camps: the chain spa — fast, fluorescent, forgettable — or the medical clinic — clinical, fluorescent, also forgettable. Both have their place. Neither is what most adults actually want.

What people seem to want is a beautiful room, soft light, a therapist who listens, and an hour where nothing is asked of them. So that's what we build. Each RelaxTouch studio is small by design — a few rooms, heated linens, a curated soundscape, and a team of licensed therapists who chose this work because they're good at it.

Our therapists

Every massage at RelaxTouch is performed by a licensed massage therapist. Our team practices Swedish, deep tissue, hot stone, sports recovery, prenatal and full-body relaxation work — and most of our therapists hold certifications in two or three modalities, so we can match the body in front of us, not the technique we happened to learn.

Our studios

You'll find us in fifteen U.S. cities, from Brooklyn and Arlington to Phoenix, Austin and the Bay Area. Each location is independent in feel — chosen for the neighborhood, designed around its light — but every studio shares the same standards: cleanliness, discretion, premium oils and linens, and a calendar built so you're never rushed in or rushed out.

Open late, open every day

We're open 9:00 AM to midnight, seven days a week. Most of the people we serve don't have nine-to-five lives, and we don't think recovery should have nine-to-five hours either.

Have a question?

The fastest way to reach a particular studio is the contact line on its page. For everything else, our central inbox is on the contact page.

pages.title: Contact RelaxTouch
pages.meta_description: Reach RelaxTouch — find direct contacts for each studio location, or the central inbox for general inquiries, press, and partnerships.
pages.content_html:
For booking, the fastest path is the contact line on each studio's location page — calls and messages go straight to the studio.

For everything else

For press, partnerships, vendor inquiries or general feedback, you can reach our central team at hello@relaxtouchtoday.com.

Find a studio

We're in fifteen U.S. cities. Find your nearest RelaxTouch studio →

A note on response times

Our studio teams respond within business hours (9 AM – midnight, daily). Central inbox replies typically come within one business day, sometimes two during peak weeks. If your message is time-sensitive — a same-day appointment, for example — please contact the studio directly.

pages.title: Privacy Policy
pages.meta_description: How RelaxTouch handles personal information you share with us — what we collect, why, and your rights.
pages.content_html:
Last updated: this policy was last revised when this site launched.

This Privacy Policy explains how RelaxTouch ("we," "our," "us") collects, uses and shares information about you when you visit relaxtouchtoday.com (the "Site") or contact one of our studios.

Information we collect

Information you give us. When you contact a studio or send us a message, you may share your name, phone number, email address, and the contents of your message. When you book a session in person, you may share information about your health, allergies, or pregnancy that helps a therapist work safely.

Information collected automatically. When you visit the Site, our hosting provider and analytics tools may log standard request information — IP address, browser type, the page you visited, and the page that referred you. We use this in aggregate to understand which content is helpful.

Cookies. The Site uses a small number of cookies for analytics and basic functionality. You can disable cookies in your browser at any time without losing access to the Site.

How we use information

We use the information you give us to respond to your inquiries, schedule and deliver your massage, and improve the Site. We do not sell your information to third parties.

Sharing

We share information only with service providers who help us run the business — for example, our hosting provider and analytics tool — and only as needed for them to do that work. If we are ever legally required to disclose information, we will do so and notify you where the law allows.

Your rights

You can ask us what information we hold about you, ask us to correct it, or ask us to delete it. Email privacy@relaxtouchtoday.com and we'll respond as quickly as we can — typically within 30 days.

Children

The Site is not intended for children under 13 and we do not knowingly collect information from children under 13.

Changes

If this policy changes, we'll update the date at the top and post the new version here.

Contact

Questions about this policy: privacy@relaxtouchtoday.com.

pages.title: Terms of Service
pages.meta_description: The terms that govern your use of relaxtouchtoday.com and our studio services.
pages.content_html:
Last updated: this document was last revised when this site launched.

By using relaxtouchtoday.com (the "Site") and our studio services, you agree to these Terms of Service. Please read them.

The service

RelaxTouch operates wellness massage studios at the locations listed on this Site. All massage services are performed by licensed massage therapists.

Bookings and cancellations

Sessions are booked directly with each studio. We ask that you give at least 24 hours' notice for cancellation or rescheduling. Late cancellations and no-shows may be charged the full session fee at the studio's discretion.

Health and safety

For your safety and the safety of our therapists, please disclose any medical conditions, recent surgeries, pregnancy, allergies (especially to nut oils), or injuries before your session. Massage is not appropriate for certain conditions; if you're unsure, ask a physician first.

Conduct

RelaxTouch provides therapeutic and wellness massage only. Inappropriate behavior of any kind will result in immediate termination of the session, charge of the full fee, and a permanent ban from all RelaxTouch studios. Our therapists are professionals; they will be treated as such.

Pricing

Prices listed on this Site reflect standard sessions: 15 minutes from $140, 30 minutes from $200, 60 minutes from $230. Add-ons and specialty modalities may be priced separately at each studio.

Site content

Articles and other content on this Site are for general information only and are not medical advice. Always consult a qualified medical professional for medical concerns.

Liability

To the fullest extent permitted by law, RelaxTouch is not liable for indirect, incidental or consequential damages arising from your use of the Site or the services. Nothing in these Terms limits liability for negligence causing personal injury where that limitation would be unlawful.

Changes

We may update these Terms from time to time. The "last updated" date above reflects the most recent revision.

Contact

Questions: hello@relaxtouchtoday.com.

locations.title: Premium Wellness Massage in Los Angeles, CA
locations.meta_description: Premium massage studio in Los Angeles, CA. Swedish, deep tissue, hot stone, sports and prenatal massage by licensed therapists. Open 9 AM to midnight, daily.
locations.intro_html:
Step into a quiet room in the middle of one of the loudest cities in the world. RelaxTouch Los Angeles is a small, intentionally restrained wellness massage studio for people who want their hour back — clean, discreet, and unhurried.

Our Los Angeles therapists are licensed and selected for skill first. Whether you're stiff from a flight into LAX, sore from a hike up Runyon, or just trying to get out of your own head for sixty minutes, the work meets you where you are.

Open every day from 9:00 AM to midnight. Late sessions, early sessions, and the gap between meetings — all at the same standard.
locations.services_html:
Sessions

  • 60-minute massage — $230. Our most-booked session. Long enough for full-body work or deep focus on a problem area.
  • 30-minute massage — $200. Targeted bodywork: neck and shoulders, back, or legs and feet.
  • 15-minute massage — $140. Quick reset between meetings or before a flight.

Modalities

Each is performed by a licensed therapist trained specifically in that style.

  • Swedish massage — long, flowing strokes for full-body relaxation. The classic, and our most-requested.
  • Deep tissue massage — slower, firmer pressure for chronic tension and muscle adhesions.
  • Hot stone therapy — basalt stones placed and used to work warmth into deep muscle. Particularly good in winter.
  • Sports recovery massage — for runners, cyclists, lifters and anyone training hard.
  • Prenatal massage — performed by therapists certified in pregnancy work, in supported side-lying position.
  • Full-body relaxation — our gentlest session, designed purely for nervous-system reset.
locations.about_studio_html:
The Los Angeles studio is small by design — a handful of rooms, soft warm light, heated linens and the kind of silence you can actually rest inside. We use premium organic oils (with a fragrance-free option), Egyptian-cotton sheets warmed before each session, and a soundscape curated for the room rather than streamed from a free playlist.

Discretion matters here. Sessions are spaced so you'll never run into the next guest, parking is easy, and the entrance is calm — not a fluorescent spa lobby. Most of our regulars work in entertainment, finance or tech and value the same thing: privacy, quality of work, and a therapist who actually listens.

If it's your first visit, plan to arrive ten minutes early to fill out a brief intake. Our therapist will ask about pressure preferences, problem areas, and anything to avoid (recent injuries, surgeries, allergies). The session ends with two minutes of stillness — most people remember that part most.

faq_json.q: How early should I arrive for my first appointment?
faq_json.a: Ten minutes is plenty. We use that time for a brief intake — pressure preference, problem areas, anything to avoid — and to get you settled into the room.
You'll undress to your level of comfort and lie under heated linens. Your therapist will only uncover the area being worked on at any moment. Many guests prefer to keep underwear on; that's fine.

faq_json.q: Do I need to book in advance?
faq_json.a: Same-day bookings are often possible, especially mid-day. Evenings, weekends and holidays book up — a day or two ahead is safer.

faq_json.q: What if I have a specific area of pain?
faq_json.a: Tell us when you book and tell the therapist again at intake. We'll plan the session around it — sometimes that means staying on one area for the full hour.
Some sessions can be booked as side-by-side in our larger room. Ask the studio when booking.
For chronic tension, weekly to bi-weekly initially. For maintenance, every three to four weeks. For pure relaxation, whenever your week needs it.
Yes. Ask the studio directly — we send digital cards by email.

Plan to arrive ten minutes early on your first visit for a brief intake. Tell us about pressure preference, problem areas, and anything to avoid — recent injuries, surgeries, or allergies. The session ends with two minutes of stillness, which most guests remember most.

faq_json.a: Ten minutes for first-time guests; five for repeat visits.
You'll undress to your level of comfort under heated linens. Your therapist only uncovers the area being worked on. Many guests prefer to keep underwear on.

faq_json.q: Do I need to book in advance?
faq_json.a: Same-day bookings are often possible mid-day. Evenings and weekends fill up — a day ahead is safer.

faq_json.q: What if I have a specific area of pain?

locations.title: Premium Wellness Massage in San Francisco, CA
locations.meta_description: Premium massage studio in San Francisco. Swedish, deep tissue, hot stone, sports and prenatal massage by licensed therapists. Open 9 AM to midnight, daily.
locations.intro_html:
San Francisco runs on screens and stairs, and most bodies arrive at the end of the day quietly braced for the next one. RelaxTouch San Francisco is a small wellness massage studio built around the opposite premise — that an hour of real stillness, in a beautiful room, is the most underrated tool in the city.

Our therapists are licensed and skilled across modalities. Sessions are unhurried, the room is quiet, and the standard does not move depending on how busy the day is.

Open every day, 9:00 AM to midnight.
locations.services_html:
Sessions

  • 60-minute massage — $230.
  • 30-minute massage — $200.
  • 15-minute massage — $140.

Modalities

  • Swedish massage — long flowing strokes for full-body relaxation.
  • Deep tissue massage — slower, firmer pressure for chronic tension and muscle adhesions, common in posture-heavy desk workers.
  • Hot stone therapy — basalt stones, deep warmth, especially welcome through SF's cold months.
  • Sports recovery massage — for runners, climbers, cyclists.
  • Prenatal massage — performed by certified prenatal therapists in side-lying position.
  • Full-body relaxation — gentle, nervous-system reset.
locations.about_studio_html:
The San Francisco studio is small, warm and quiet — heated linens, premium organic oils, soft light and a soundscape curated for the room. We're easy to reach by car or transit and we keep the schedule spaced so you'll never overlap with the next guest.

Arrive ten minutes early on your first visit for a brief intake — pressure preference, problem areas, anything to avoid (recent injuries, surgeries, allergies). The therapist plans the session from there. Sessions end with two minutes of stillness; most guests remember that part most.

faq_json.a: Ten minutes for first-time guests; five for repeat visits.
Undress to your level of comfort under heated linens. The therapist only uncovers the area being worked on.

faq_json.q: Do I need to book in advance?
faq_json.a: Mid-day same-day bookings are often possible. Evenings and weekends — a day or two ahead is safer.

faq_json.q: What if I have neck and shoulder pain from a desk?
faq_json.a: Mention it at intake — for many SF guests this is the entire session, and that's perfectly fine.
Weekly to bi-weekly for chronic tension; every three to four weeks for maintenance.

faq_json.a: Most-requested combination here is 60 minutes Swedish with focused work on calves, lower back and neck.
Yes — we book up to 11 PM and the session itself can run until midnight.
Mid-day often works; evenings fill up — a day ahead is safer.
Weekly to bi-weekly for chronic tension; monthly for maintenance.

Open 9 AM to midnight, every day.
